full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding exam constraints and eligibility rules.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Read the midsem Part A instructions carefully and explain all constraints regarding venue, year, algorithm category, and reproducibility.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me clearly understand the CORE A* requirement, 2009–2012 year restriction, and CPU-only reproducibility constraints.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I rechecked the official PDF instructions and verified the year range and allowed venues myself.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Searching for ARIMA-based papers not selected by classmates.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Suggest ARIMA-based CORE A* papers between 2009–2012 that are different from the already submitted list.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided multiple ARIMA/ARMA related papers and helped narrow down unique options.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-checked the live response sheet to ensure the suggested paper was not already selected.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verifying ARMA relation to ARIMA requirement.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Does ARMA count under ARIMA for the exam requirement?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that ARMA is a special case of ARIMA when differencing parameter d=0.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I confirmed from my time-series notes that ARIMA(p,d,q) reduces to ARMA(p,q) when d=0.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is 'On the Consistency of ARMA Model Selection' considered a methodological contribution?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained that the paper contributes to model selection methodology through theoretical consistency analysis.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I read the abstract and introduction to confirm the focus on model selection criteria.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can this ARMA model selection paper be reproduced using synthetic datasets on a CPU-only laptop?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that ARMA time series can be simulated and AIC/BIC comparison experiments can be implemented in Python.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I verified that synthetic ARMA data can be generated using statsmodels in Python.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Check if anyone in the provided submission list selected this ARMA consistency paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that no classmate selected this specific JMLR 2011 paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I manually scanned the submission spreadsheet for the exact title and authors.
